Terraform used the selected providers to generate the following execution plan. Resource actions are
indicated with the following symbols:
  + create
  ~ update in-place
-/+ destroy and then create replacement
 <= read (data resources)

Terraform will perform the following actions:

  # module.app.data.aws_ami.base will be read during apply
  # (depends on a resource or a module with changes pending)
 <= data "aws_ami" "base" {
      + id = (known after apply)
    }

  # aws_instance.web will be created
  + resource "aws_instance" "web" {
      + ami = "ami-123"
    }

  # aws_instance.old will be destroyed
  - resource "aws_instance" "old" {
      - ami = "ami-000" -> null
    }

  # aws_security_group.sg must be replaced
-/+ resource "aws_security_group" "sg" {
      ~ name = "a" -> "b" # forces replacement
      ~ tags = [
          - "old",
        ]
    }

  # aws_thing.cfg will be updated in-place
  ~ resource "aws_thing" "cfg" {
      ~ input = "x" -> "y"
    }

 # aws_kms_alias.k will no longer be managed by Terraform, but will not be destroyed
 # (destroy = false is set in the configuration)
 . resource "aws_kms_alias" "k" {
    }

Changes to Outputs:
  + new_output = "v"
  - old_output = "x" -> null
  ~ chg_output = "a" -> "b"

Plan: 1 to add, 2 to change, 2 to destroy.
